prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and high seasonality variance (0.61), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The trend champion ONLY_DWT_rf excels at tracking slope changes, while FT_svr best captures seasonality. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon ones, but the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination as the optimal method for robust ensemble performance.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_rf", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_catboost", "rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ARIMA", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["svr", "ETS", "THETA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3896,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and high seasonality variance (0.61), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The trend champion ONLY_DWT_rf excels at tracking slope changes, while FT_svr best captures seasonality. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon ones, but the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination as the optimal method for robust ensemble performance."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": ["inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.70"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity (2 baseline, 2 selection, 1 weighted). Adding inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.70 from SMAPE leaderboard (score 1.0309544427080943) improves SMAPE performance. No leakage issues - all strategies use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. No redundant candidates to prune.",
  "changes": ["Added inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.70 for better SMAPE"],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and high seasonality variance (0.61), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The trend champion ONLY_DWT_rf excels at tracking slope changes, while FT_svr best captures seasonality. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon ones, but the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination as the optimal method for robust ensemble performance.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity per horizon. relative_spread_mean=2.49 (>>0.25) signals strong disagreement favoring robust methods like dba_combination and trimmed_mean_r0.20. n_windows=3 ≤4 requires small top_k=3 with shrinkage≥0.3 for stability. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality_variance=0.61 and trend_champion ONLY_DWT_rf excelling at slope tracking while FT_svr captures seasonality.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity (2 baseline, 2 selection, 1 weighted). Adding inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.70 from SMAPE leaderboard (score 1.0309544427080943) improves SMAPE performance. No leakage issues - all strategies use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. No redundant candidates to prune.
